Bridges TVL Relative — The TVL dominance of each target-blockchain bridge, expressed as that bridge's TVL divided by the total TVL across all bridges. Data by Glassnode.

Bridges TVL — The total USD value locked inside the Ethereum side of bridge smart contracts, broken out per destination blockchain. Locked tokens are unavailable on Ethereum and available on the targe
Realized Profit/Loss Ratio — Realized Profit/Loss Ratio is the ratio between all coins moved at a profit and at a loss, computed as Realized Profit / Realized Loss. Data by Glassnode.

Realized Profits-to-Value (RPV) Ratio — Realized Profits-to-Value (RPV) Ratio is the ratio of Realized Profits to Realized Cap, comparing profit-taking in the market with its overall cost basis on a d
Realized Profit — Realized Profit is the total profit (USD value) of all moved coins whose price at their last movement was lower than the price at the current movement. Data by Glassnode.
Realized Loss — Realized Loss is the total USD loss across all moved coins whose price at their last movement was higher than the price at the current movement. Data by Glassnode.

Pi Cycle Top Indicator — Pi Cycle Top Indicator is composed of the 111-day simple moving average (111SMA) and a 2x multiple of the 350-day simple moving average (350SMA x 2) of the asset's price. Data

Network Value to Transactions Signal — NVT Signal (NVTS) is a modified version of the original NVT Ratio that uses a 90-day moving average of the daily transaction volume in the denominator instead of
Network Value to Transactions Ratio (NVT) — Network Value to Transactions Ratio (NVT) is market cap divided by the transferred on-chain volume measured in USD. Data by Glassnode.

Long Term Holder NUPL — Long Term Holder NUPL (LTH-NUPL) is Net Unrealized Profit/Loss computed on UTXOs with a lifespan of at least 155 days, serving as an indicator of the behaviour of long-term inv
Net Unrealized Profit/Loss (NUPL) — Net Unrealized Profit/Loss (NUPL) is the difference between Relative Unrealized Profit and Relative Unrealized Loss. Data by Glassnode.
Net Realized Profit/Loss — Net Realized Profit/Loss is the net profit or loss of all moved coins, defined as Realized Profit minus Realized Loss. Data by Glassnode.
Median Spent Output Lifespan (MSOL) — Median Spent Output Lifespan (MSOL) is the median age (in days) of spent transaction outputs. Data by Glassnode.
Liveliness — Liveliness is the ratio of the sum of Coin Days Destroyed to the sum of all coin days ever created. Data by Glassnode.

Investor Capitalization — Investor Capitalization is the difference between Realized Cap and Thermocap, discounting the capital paid to miners from the market's general cost basis and serving as a bot

Fear & Greed Index — Fear & Greed Index is a third-party sentiment composite from Alternative.me that compresses inputs from multiple sources into a single 0-to-100 scalar capturing investor sentiment
Dormancy Flow — Dormancy Flow is the ratio of the current market capitalization to the annualized dormancy value (measured in USD). Data by Glassnode.
Difficulty Ribbon Compression — Difficulty Ribbon Compression is a market indicator that uses a normalized standard deviation to quantify compression of the Difficulty Ribbon. Data by Glassnode.

Difficulty Ribbon — Difficulty Ribbon is an indicator built from seven simple moving averages (200d, 128d, 90d, 60d, 40d, 25d, 14d) of Bitcoin mining difficulty, stacked as a ribbon. Data by Glassnode
Cumulative Value-Days Destroyed (CVDD) — Cumulative Value-Days Destroyed (CVDD) is the ratio of the cumulative USD value of Coin Days Destroyed to the market age in days. Data by Glassnode.
Coin Years Destroyed — Coin Years Destroyed (CYD) is the 365-day rolling sum of Coin Days Destroyed (CDD), the amount of coin days destroyed over the trailing year. Data by Glassnode.
Coin Blocks Destroyed — Coin Blocks Destroyed for any given transaction is calculated by taking the number of coins in a transaction and multiplying it by the number of blocks it has been since those

Supply-Adjusted CDD — Supply-Adjusted CDD is Coin Days Destroyed divided by circulating supply (the total amount of coins issued). Data by Glassnode.

Coin Days Destroyed (CDD) — Coin Days Destroyed (CDD) measures the volume-weighted age of coins spent on a given day. Data by Glassnode.

Supply-Adjusted Dormancy — Supply-Adjusted Dormancy is the supply-normalized form of Average Coin Dormancy, the average number of days destroyed per coin transacted, defined as the ratio of coin days
Average Spent Output Lifespan (ASOL) — Average Spent Output Lifespan (ASOL) is the average age, in days, of spent transaction outputs. Data by Glassnode.
Average Coin Dormancy — Average Coin Dormancy is the average number of days destroyed per coin transacted, defined as the ratio of coin days destroyed to total transfer volume. Data by Glassnode.
Number of Entities With Balance ≥ 1k — The number of unique entities holding at least 1,000 coins. Data by Glassnode.
Entities Supply Distribution — The relative distribution of the circulating supply held by entities, broken down by balance band. Data by Glassnode.

Miner Net Position Change — The 30-day change of the supply held in miner addresses. Data by Glassnode.
Balance in Miner Wallets — The total supply held in miner addresses. Data by Glassnode.

Percent Balance on Exchanges — The percentage of supply held on exchange addresses. Data by Glassnode.

Slot Height — The slot height, i.e. the total number of slots ever included in the main blockchain. Data by Glassnode.
ETH 2.0 Number of New Deposits — The number of transactions depositing 32 ETH into the ETH 2.0 deposit contract. Data by Glassnode.

Stake Effectiveness — Stake Effectiveness is the proportion of staked ETH actively participating in consensus, computed as the ratio of Total Effective Balance to Total Staked Balance of the Proof-of-
Slashing Event Count — The total number of slashing events recorded on the blockchain. Data by Glassnode.
Participation Rate — A measure of network validator responsiveness, tracking the proportion of available slots in which the assigned proposer successfully produced a block. Data by Glassnode.
Orphaned Blocks — The total number of orphaned blocks, i.e. blocks not selected as part of the canonical chain. Data by Glassnode.
Missed Blocks — The total number of slots in which a validator failed to produce a block. Data by Glassnode.

Estimated Annual Issuance ROI per validator — Estimated Annual Issuance ROI per Validator is the theoretical ETH-denominated annual return (%) on a 32-ETH stake validator, with ETH issuance determined
Estimated Annual Issuance — The theoretical annual ETH issuance curve of the Ethereum Proof-of-Stake network, determined by the number of validators participating in consensus. Data by Glassnode.
Epoch Height — The epoch height, i.e. the total number of epochs ever included in the main blockchain. Data by Glassnode.
Staking Deposits — The number of new 32-ETH stake deposits made into the ETH 2.0 staking contract. Data by Glassnode.
Total Effective Balance — The total staked balance actively participating in Proof-of-Stake consensus. Data by Glassnode.
ETH 2.0 Total Value Staked by Provider — The total amount of ETH transferred to the ETH 2.0 deposit contract, broken down by the staking provider that routed each deposit. Data by Glassnode.
Attestation Count — The number of "yes" votes cast by the validator committee attesting to the validity of the block produced in each slot. Data by Glassnode.
Active Validators — The count of active validators. Data by Glassnode.
